title: ActuationEvent
description: Generic event archetype for actuation results (Actuator → Sensor). Emitted by an Actuator's Effector after performing a state mutation on the target StateObjectArchetype. Carries the mutation outcome: whether it succeeded, what instances were affected, and what properties were written. The Sensor's Receptor receives this event as observation input (cybernetic flow step 5). This schema is the base contract for ActuationEventSchema as referenced in GSM Sensor functional rules ('For every event archetype E whose schema is a subtype of ActuationEventSchema, the sensor MUST have a receptor bound to E'). SIE-seeded: one instance per SIE tenant, reused across all Actuations.

correlationId.description: Correlation identifier shared across the actuation chain. Same value as the originating ActuationControlEvent.correlationId, enabling end-to-end tracing.

actuationType.description: The kind of state mutation that was performed. Echoed from the ActuationControlEvent.

stateObjectArchetype.description: Name of the StateObjectArchetype that was mutated.

status.description: Outcome of the mutation execution. SUCCESS: all targeted instances mutated as commanded. FAILURE: no mutation applied (rollback or error). PARTIAL: some instances mutated, others failed (only for multi-instance MODIFICATION/DELETION).

affectedInstanceIds.description: Identifiers of the state object instances affected by the mutation. For CREATION: the ID(s) of newly created instance(s). For MODIFICATION/DELETION: the ID(s) of mutated/deleted instance(s). For ACQUISITION: the ID(s) of acquired (read) instance(s). Empty array on total FAILURE.

writtenProperties.description: Property names that were actually written during the mutation. For CREATION: all initial properties set. For MODIFICATION: only updated properties. For DELETION/ACQUISITION: empty array. Corresponds to Actuation.writtenProperties in the GSM definition.

error.description: Error details when status is FAILURE or PARTIAL. Null on SUCCESS.

failedInstanceIds.description: Instance IDs that failed mutation (PARTIAL status only).

actuationId.description: Reference to the GSM Actuation definition.

ruleId.description: Reference to the GSM Rule whose body triggered the actuation.

actuatorId.description: Reference to the Actuator mechanism that executed the mutation.
